calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2025 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION's MOTION FOR IMPOSITION OF SANCTIONS.

Defendant Federal Express Corporation's motion for sanctions pursuant to CCP 128.7 is denied. Sanctions under 128.7 are statutory; they lie only where an attorney's implied certification of the matters listed in 128.7(b) are false or lack reasonable support. FedEx's sanctions request essentially makes the case that opposing counsel's pleadings are so frequently conclusory and lacking necessary ultimate facts that the court must conclude that these conclusory pleadings, including the complaint initially filed here, are "being presented primarily for an improper purpose, such as to harass or to cause unnecessary delay or needless increase in the cost of litigation." (CCP 128.7(b)(1).) The range in factual detail of pleadings filed in this court is so broad that the court cannot say based on conclusory pleadings alone that counsel's purpose must have been improper. The court also denies plaintiff Andrea Anderson's cross-motion for sanctions.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT FEDERAL EXPRESS CORPORATION's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT.

The issue on demurrer is, taking the facts properly pleaded and properly noticed as true, does the challenged cause of action necessarily fail to state a cause for relief. (See Code Civ. Proc., section 430.10(e); Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318.) In assessing whether the complaint states a cause of action, the court accepts all properly pleaded material facts, but not contentions, deductions, or conclusions of fact or law. (Minton v. Dignity Health (2019), 39 Cal.App.5th 1155, 1161.) "[I]f, on consideration of all facts stated, it appears that plaintiff is entitled to any relief against defendant, the complaint will be held good, though facts may not be clearly stated, or may be intermingled with a statement of other facts irrelevant to cause of action shown, or though plaintiff may demand relief to which he is not entitled under facts alleged." (Augustine v. Trucco (1954) 124 Cal.App.2d 229, 236, quoting Matteson v. Wagoner (1905) 147 Cal. 739, 742.)

Defendant Federal Express Corporation's demurrer to the entire complaint is sustained with leave to amend. The complaint solely contains conclusions of fact and fails to establish even basic facts alleging an employment relationship, when the incidents occurred, or which parties were involved. The amended complaint shall be filed no later than April 1, 2025.

Order signed.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
